seoTitle: Macclesfield vs Macgillivray — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Macclesfield and Macgillivray. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Macclesfield and Macgillivray.
heading: Macclesfield vs Macgillivray — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Macclesfield, South Australia and Macgillivray, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Macclesfield has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Macgillivray (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Macclesfield or Macgillivray safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Macclesfield has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Macgillivray scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Macclesfield scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Macgillivray scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Macclesfield or Macgillivray?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Macclesfield has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
